title: CLARITY Act clears Senate Banking Committee
content:
Scott Melker discusses Senate passing the CLARITY Act with a 15-9 vote, Jerome Powell stepping down, and other big crypto headlines of the day.

"The Daily Wolf with Scott Melker" airs every day at 12:00 p.m. Tune in for your daily dose of all things crypto.

Make sure to also check out Yahoo Finance's new crypto hub to find the latest crypto-related news.

Video Transcript

00:00 Scott Melker

The clarity Act markup has officially passed through the Senate Banking Committee, meaning now it needs to merge with the agriculture Committee bill and then hopefully head to the Senate floor. Meanwhile, we have three companies closing their convertible notes and debt and looking for ways to add more Bitcoin to the balance sheet. Three different companies, three different strategies. We're going to talk about that. and of course, the sun setting of one Jerome Powell.

00:26 Scott Melker

Let's go.

00:54 Scott Melker

What is up everybody? Welcome to the daily Wolf on Yahoo Finance. I'm your host Scott Melker, also known as the Wolf of all streets. We're going to spend the next 15 minutes diving into everything that's happening in crypto markets right now. It's the perfect day to put everything in context because of course we have Jerome Powell in his last day at the Fed, but we will get to that at the end. Obviously, the biggest and main story today is one we've been covering every single day.

01:21 Scott Melker

The US Senate Committee advances crypto bill in milestone for digital assets. As I told you yesterday, this was not without its controversy, without its mayhem, without its viral clips and certainly without Elizabeth Warren absolutely losing her mind repeatedly. But what's interesting here is we expected a vote of 1311, a partisan vote with Republicans voting for and Democrats voting again but against, but it ended up actually getting 15 votes for with also Brooks and Gallego both joining the Republican

02:07 Scott Melker

cause. So now we can say that this is a bipartisan passage of the clarity Act from the Senate Banking Committee. But that's not really the story. Because what really happened here is they realized that it was time. They've done enough work on this. It's been pending for months and they were going to pass this version once again with the ethics clause still pending. So both Gallego and also Brooks said they still are not going to vote for a version of this that hits the Senate floor

02:49 Scott Melker

that does not have an ethics clause that bans insiders including the president and his family from participating and enriching themselves from crypto markets. So, just to be clear, once again, now this has passed the Senate Banking Committee. So the chairman of the Senate Banking Committee and Agriculture Committee and their minions need to get together now and somehow merge this into one coherent bill, which is not the easiest thing in the world and not something that we can take for granted will get done.

03:22 Scott Melker

Especially because we know that we are running up against time. We have Memorial Day effectively in a week. and after that, we are going to have a recess and then we have July 4th and then it's midterm season. If this doesn't all somehow fall perfectly into place over the next month and a half, it's likely not going to get done at all. So they've got their work cut out for them. If we do get this merged and one cohesive bill hitting the floor, then it hits the Senate floor where it will actually be debated and voted on and we know that there will be a lot of opposition and that the bank lobby will still be coming in hot.

04:09 Scott Melker

They literally wrote 8,000 letters to their senators in a matter of days ahead of this markup trying to block this bill. I told you last week that they were on Sunday of Mother's Day scrambling to get this done to try to oppose this bill. So very hard for me to imagine it passing even though now poly market has it at over 70%. And I mean, you know, in other news, Elizabeth Warren literally went out on a limb and said if this passes, it's going to blow up the entire economy.

04:47 Scott Melker

Because we are prone to hyperbole and having our heads explode on the floor of the Senate. So that's the first story today, which we will obviously be tracking massively. The second story here is interesting because we have three companies with three different strategies. No, no, no pun intended there. on how to proceed with buying more Bitcoin. We're going to show you the first one right here. This was news yesterday. Actually, my buddy Matt Cole over there at Strive who I interviewed last week at consensus,

05:25 Scott Melker

he rang the bell on the Nasdaq this morning to celebrate this. But the news was that Strive Seda sets US first with daily 13% Bitcoin backed dividend preferred. So there was a lot to this. This came in their earnings call this week. So much like STRC, which I've told you about at length from strategy and Michael Sailor, we have Strive Seda. Now this offers a 13% yield, which is a bit higher obviously than what STRC is offering at 11.5%. But now they will be the first

06:06 Scott Melker

listed security in the United States to pay cash dividends every single business day. So obviously that 13% when you get paid daily is going to compound faster over a monthly or yearly rate over 14% and you still get the tax benefit much like STRC of having this be a return of capital rather than a capital gain. So, the other big story here is that in the quarter, they retired all of their debt. Strive is entirely debt free here. They own over 15,000 Bitcoin and they have stated that Seda will be the way

07:00 Scott Melker

that they continue moving forward to buy more Bitcoin. Now, I told you last week that Marathon was basically becoming an AI company that was going to mine some Bitcoin. They sold $1.5 billion worth of Bitcoin to close their convertible notes from last year and to re tool their balance sheet to be ready for AI to become basically an AI data center. So their strategy was actually to sell some Bitcoin. To be clear, Strive did not sell Bitcoin. They issued, they they issued their stock and they used cash reserves

07:44 Scott Melker

and used other tools to get this done. So, those are two different strategies but both wanting to clear all of their debt. And now, the next story obviously is this guy, strategy right here, Michael Sailor, strategy to repurchase 1.5 billion of 2029 convertible notes. This is really interesting. So, as I've told you, there's been an evolution with Michael Sailor of how to structure things to buy more Bitcoin. And if you've listened to him and as he's told me in our interviews in the past,

08:29 Scott Melker

he's really arrived at the conclusion that the best way is through STRC. But he has all these other convertible notes and products that he's released in the past that he wants to close down. Now, interestingly, he has a lot of cash as well, still in the Treasury. He could probably close this note with it and they're going to get an 8% discount on it. And he could also sell some Bitcoin. or, you know, do some financial engineering on the stock of micro strategy. but in their disclosure, they said that they could sell Bitcoin to do this. This is part of that new evolution of

09:12 Scott Melker

be a net buyer instead of saying never sell. But I think the bigger story here actually is that all of these companies, whichever way they're doing it, they want to clear out those convertible notes, which was the hot on fire way for them to buy Bitcoin in the past. So Seda is Strive's version of STRC, STRC is strategies version. Both of them want that to be the only way or the major way that they are going to be buying Bitcoin into the future. I mean, meanwhile, we saw

09:59 Scott Melker

STRC once again just put in absolutely monster record volumes over the past few days and it was over par. Now it's trading back down to $99 now that it's past X dividend day. And so, Michael Sailor able to buy arguably another 9, 10, 11, 12,000 Bitcoin this week. We will see that announcement on Monday. But the Bitcoin buying machine was massive. I do think that that was part of the push up to $82,000. People were probably pre-empting that move that's very transparent coming from Sailor to take advantage of that trade.

10:48 Scott Melker

And I think that that was just an incredible trade that you can make and it's going to be one you're going to be able to make into the future. And now we have to get on to the last story of the day and probably the biggest. Jerome Powell rides off into the sunset this week. Eight years, two presidents and one major pandemic. 40 year high inflation and the most extraordinary monetary policy experiment in the history of the Federal Reserve. Kevin Warsh has the keys now. So before we close the book on the Powell era, let's roll the tape.

11:21 Scott Melker

Because if you remembered every Powell prediction and traded the opposite, congratulations, you're rich. This is the Powell Hall of Shame. December 2018. Powell hikes rates four times into Trump's economy. The market is begging for mercy and then Powell goes on stage and tells the world the balance sheet runoff is, quote, on autopilot.

11:51 Jerome Powell

So we thought carefully about this, um, how to normalize policy and came to the view that we would effectively had have the balance sheet run off on automatic pilot and use monetary policy, rate rate policy to adjust to incoming data.

12:15 Scott Melker

The market heard autopilot and threw itself right out of the plane. Worst Christmas Eve in the history of the S&P 500. Two weeks later, Powe folds like a lawn chair on a panel in Atlanta.

12:30 Jerome Powell

As always, there is no preset path for policy. And particularly with the muted inflation readings that we've seen coming in, we will be patient as we watch to see how the economy evolves. If we ever came to the conclusion that uh any aspect of our normalization plans was was somehow interfering with our achievement of our statutory goals, we wouldn't hesitate to change it.

13:00 Scott Melker

The Powe pivot is born. Lesson one guys, don't trust forward guidance ever.

13:10 Jerome Powell

You know, we're not thinking about raising rates. We're not even thinking about thinking about raising rates.

13:15 Scott Melker

Then came the magic word. Transitory.

13:21 Jerome Powell

So I think the word transitory has different meanings to different people to

13:26 Scott Melker

He said it in March, said it in June, said it in September. Muhammad El-Aan called it the worst inflation call in the history of the Federal Reserve.

13:37 Mohamed A. El-Erian

So it is a is going to go down in history as one of the worst inflation calls um by the Federal Reserve.

13:48 Scott Melker

Then May 2022, right before his second confirmation, Powell looks the world in the eye and says 75 basis points is off the table.

14:02 Jerome Powell

75 basis point, uh, in an increase is not something the committee is actively considering.

14:09 Scott Melker

Four jumbo hikes in a row. The largest tightening cycle in 40 years. So much for forward guidance. Again, Act three, March 7th, 2023. Powell sits before the Senate Banking Committee, two-hour testimony, talks about inflation, the labor market, monetary policy. What does he not talk about? Banking stress, interest rate risk on bank balance sheets. The fact that the second largest bank failure in American history is 48 hours away. Just two days after Powell's testimony, Silicon Valley Bank suffers a 42

14:48 Scott Melker

billion dollar bank run. The next day, it's gone. Signature bank follows. First Republic follows. Three of the four largest bank failures in US history. all on Jerome Powell's Watch. Two weeks later, Powe stands at the podium and says the banking system is sound and resilient.

15:13 Jerome Powell

Our banking system is sound and resilient with strong capital and liquidity.

15:23 Scott Melker

Same line, different week. One catastrophic banking crisis in between, sound, resilient, and about to require the largest emergency liquidity facility since 2008. Act four, February 2024, Scott Pelly asks Powe directly, was the Fed too slow on inflation?

15:52 Scott Pelley

Was the Fed too slow to recognize inflation in 2021?

16:01 Jerome Powell

So in hindsight it would have been better to to to have tightened policy earlier.

16:09 Scott Melker

Happy to say that. Six months later, Jackson Hole, Powe takes the podium and delivers the most Powe sentence ever uttered.

16:19 Jerome Powell

The good ship transitory was a crowded one.

16:25 Scott Melker

The good ship transitory was a crowded one. Translation Wolf pack, everyone else was wrong too. Don't blame me, that's not accountability, that's a press release. Then September, seven weeks before the election, strong economy and inflation still above target. Powe cuts 50 basis points anyway.

16:49 Jerome Powell

The Federal Open market Committee decided to reduce the degree of policy restraint by lowering our policy interest rate by a half percentage point.

17:02 Scott Melker

Jumbo. Out of the gate, into a fire that wasn't out yet. By April of 2025, inflation's back to 3.8%, the highest in two years. You can't make this up. Act 5, Trump comes back and Trump has receipts.

17:21 Donald Trump

I mean, Scott, you got to work on this guy. He's got some real mental problems. No, he's something wrong with him. It's just you did. I I'd be honest, I'd love to fire his ass. You got to work on him, Scott. I'll be picking a new head of the Fed soon. Our Fed person is absolutely despite him, we have numbers that really nobody's ever seen. Vince, right?

17:59 Scott Melker

So we're taking a look and it looks like it's about 3.1 billion. It went up a little bit or a lot. So the 2.7 is now 3.1. I'm not aware of that. It just came out. I haven't heard that from anybody. It just came out. Well, he's he's he's billions of dollars over budget. So he either is incompetent or he's crooked. I don't know what he is, but he doesn't certainly he doesn't do a very good job.

18:31 Scott Melker

Trump called him every name in the book, then opened a DOJ investigation over a two and a half billion dollar renovation of the Fed building. and Powell, a political measured Central Bank J had to publicly defend himself on whether there were marble bathrooms in his office.

18:50 Jerome Powell

There's no VPI dining room. There's no new marble. We took down the old marble, we're putting it back up. We'll have to use new marble where some of the old marble broke, but

19:02 Scott Melker

That's the chair of the Federal Reserve defending his elevators. The empire ends not with a bang, but with a question about Wayne Scotting. And now, Kevin Warsh, confirmed this week, 54 to 45. The most divisive Fed chair vote in history. Warsh has been Powell's loudest public critic. He wrote an op-ed in the Wall Street Journal calling Powell's record one of quote unwise choices. He went on CNBC and called for, and I love this, regime change at the Federal Reserve. Regime change at the Central Bank.

19:40 Scott Melker

We've officially entered a new era. So here's the question. Was Powell the worst Fed chair in history? Peter Navarro thinks so. Trump definitely thinks so. The Heritage Foundation already crowned him. Or did he pull off the impossible? A soft landing after the worst inflation shock in 40 years, no recession, unemployment under 4% the whole way down. You decide. But understand this, every Fed chair makes mistakes. How just happened to be on camera, every single one of them.

20:19 Scott Melker

New Sheriff, new regime, same Fed, same dual mandate, same impossible job. We'll see if Warsh does it better or just does it differently. I'm Scott Melker. This is the Daily Wolf.

title: Strategy Dividend Shift Puts Bitcoin Funding And Valuation In Fresh Focus
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Strategy Inc is proposing to shift dividend payouts on its Stretch Preferred Stock (STRC) from monthly to semi monthly. STRC has recently recorded record trading volume, reflecting heightened activity in the preferred stock. The changes sit at the core of how Strategy funds its Bitcoin buying program using STRC as a key financing tool.

For investors watching NasdaqGS:MSTR, these moves come on top of a share price of $186.97 and a mixed return profile. The stock is up 4.0% over the past week and 36.1% over the past month, while year to date it is up 19.0%. Over a 1 year period the stock has fallen 52.9%, but over 3 years it is up by a very large amount and over 5 years it has gained 295.6%. This underlines how sensitive the story is to timing.

The planned shift to semi monthly STRC dividends and the surge in trading volume sharpen the focus on how sustainable Strategy’s Bitcoin funding engine is for both preferred and common shareholders. As the preferred stock grows in importance, investors may increasingly weigh liquidity, timing of cash flows, and the interplay between STRC and MSTR when assessing overall exposure to the company’s Bitcoin centric model.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Strategy by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Strategy.NasdaqGS:MSTR Earnings & Revenue Growth as at May 2026

Is Strategy's dividend sustainable? Check out what every dividend investor needs to know in our dividend analysis.

Quick Assessment

✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At US$186.97, MSTR trades about 51% below the consensus analyst target of US$380.43. ❌ Simply Wall St Valuation: Shares are flagged as around 13.8% above estimated fair value, so the stock screens as overvalued on this model. ✅ Recent Momentum: The stock is up 36.1% over the past 30 days, showing strong short term momentum into this preferred stock news.

There is only one way to know the right time to buy, sell or hold Strategy. Head to Simply Wall St's company report for the latest analysis of Strategy's Fair Value.

Key Considerations

📊 The shift to semi monthly STRC dividends and record volume puts a spotlight on how much of Strategy's Bitcoin exposure is funded via preferred stock rather than common equity. 📊 Watch STRC trading liquidity, any change in preferred issuance and the gap between the US$186.97 share price and the US$380.43 analyst target when sizing exposure. ⚠️ The identified risk of shareholder dilution remains important, given the role of equity and preferred instruments in funding the Bitcoin buying program.

Story Continues

title: Forget MicroStrategy. The Company Taking a Cut Every Time Bitcoin Traders Panic Is Up 7% This Year and Pays a $5 Dividend
content:
Quick Read

MicroStrategy (MSTR) posted a $12.44B net loss in Q4 2025 with quarterly software revenue of just $123M against a $61B market cap, while operating as a leveraged bitcoin proxy that raised $25.3B in capital during 2025 to accumulate digital assets. CME Group (CME) posted Q1 2026 revenue of $1.88B (up 14.5% YoY), net income up 20% to $1.15B, and returned $3.9B in dividends during 2025, with all six asset classes hitting quarterly highs and operating margin of 69.8%. MicroStrategy’s stock movements are driven by bitcoin holdings and accounting rather than software fundamentals, making CME a structurally superior alternative for investors seeking real earnings, cash returns, and exposure to growing derivatives trading volumes across all asset classes. The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and CME Group wasn't one of them. Get them here FREE.

MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), now operating as Strategy, is once again the loudest ticker in the room, riding a 55.97% one-month rally and a 12.79% weekly pop on the back of yet another bitcoin treasury announcement. But here is what you should actually be watching.

Strip away the bitcoin accounting and there is barely a company underneath. Q4 2025 produced a $12.44 billion net loss and diluted EPS of -$42.93, driven almost entirely by a $17.44 billion unrealized loss on digital assets under ASU 2023-08. Quarterly software revenue was just $122.99M against a market cap north of $61 billion. The price-to-sales ratio sits at 134.95, operating margin is -44.02%, and beta is 3.595. CEO Phong Le openly described the model as raising "$25.3 billion of capital in 2025" to buy more bitcoin, with another $8.1 billion in common ATM and $29+ billion in preferred ATM capacity still queued. The STRC preferred carries an 11.25% dividend rate. Polymarket traders price a 41.5% chance MSTR sells some bitcoin by year-end and a 27.5% chance of MSCI delisting. This is a leveraged bitcoin proxy wearing a software ticker.

The redirect is the house that takes a cut every time MSTR's traders sweat: CME Group (NASDAQ:CME). It owns the volatility without owning the asset.

The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and CME Group wasn't one of them.Get them here FREE.

Three reasons CME stands out versus MSTR for long-horizon investors

1. Records across every asset class, not just crypto. Q1 2026 delivered revenue of $1.88 billion (up 14.5% YoY), GAAP net income up 20% to $1.15 billion, and a record 36.2 million contracts in average daily volume, up 22% YoY. All six asset classes, interest rates, equity indexes, FX, energy, agricultural commodities and metals, hit quarterly highs. Crypto exposure flows through 24/7 cryptocurrency futures and options, keeping the asset off CME's balance sheet.

Story Continues

2. Cash returns instead of dilution. CME paid roughly $3.9 billion in 2025 dividends and has returned about $30 billion to shareholders since 2012 under its variable dividend policy, and bought back $536 million of stock in Q1 2026 alone. That is the structural opposite of MSTR, which is the largest U.S. equity issuer for the second consecutive year.

3. Real earnings, defensive profile. CME trades at a forward P/E of 24x, return on equity of 15.9%, operating margin of 69.8%, and a beta of 0.26, with 91.7% institutional ownership. CEO Terry Duffy summed up the setup: "In a world in which risk has become the new normal, 2026 is off to a record-breaking start."

For a retirement-focused investor, the choice is between a -51.65% one-year drawdown in MSTR plus perpetual preferred obligations, or CME's 7.59% YTD grind backed by a $5.05 dividend. Even Block (NYSE:XYZ), with 54% projected 2026 EPS growth, still booked a $234 million bitcoin remeasurement loss in Q4. CME has none of that noise.

What to watch: CME belongs near the top of any derivatives-exchange research list this week, while MSTR's headline-driven moves continue to track bitcoin rather than software fundamentals.

The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks

title: Is It Time To Reassess MicroStrategy (MSTR) After Its Recent Share Price Surge?
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

If you are asking whether Strategy's current share price lines up with its underlying value, this article is designed to walk you through the key pieces you need to weigh. Strategy closed at US$184.42, with the stock down 1.3% over the last week, up 43.4% over the last month, up 17.3% year to date, down 56.3% over the last year, and showing a very large gain over 3 and 5 years. Recent coverage has focused on Strategy's sharp swings in investor sentiment and how that has influenced trading activity, with attention on whether the stock's long term track record still justifies its current pricing. Commentators have also highlighted how broader interest in software stocks has fed into the recent share price moves, which is important context when you assess valuation. On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Strategy scores 2 out of 6. The rest of this article will compare what different valuation methods indicate about the stock's pricing and will point you toward a more complete way to assess value at the end.

Strategy scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Strategy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes a series of projected future cash flows and discounts them back to today, in order to estimate what those future streams are worth in present dollar terms.

For Strategy, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of about $72.0 million. Analyst and extrapolated projections then move to positive territory, with forecast free cash flow of about $3.6 billion by 2028. Intermediate years in the model include a projected loss of $103 million in 2026, followed by projected free cash flow of around $3.5 billion in 2027 and $3.6 billion in 2028, with further years extrapolated from those inputs.

Discounting these projected cash flows back to today produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $164.83 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $184.42, the DCF output indicates Strategy is about 11.9% above this estimate, so the stock screens as overvalued on this model alone.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Strategy may be overvalued by 11.9%. Discover 44 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.MSTR Discounted Cash Flow as at May 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Strategy.

Story Continues

Approach 2: Strategy Price vs Book

For companies where profitability can be uneven, the price to book ratio, or P/B, is often a useful cross check because it compares the share price to the accounting value of net assets. Investors usually accept a higher P/B when they expect stronger growth and lower risk, and a lower P/B when growth is uncertain or risks are higher.

Strategy currently trades on a P/B of 1.77x. That sits below the broader Software industry average of about 2.62x and also below the peer group average of 10.77x. On those simple comparisons alone, the stock screens as cheaper than both its sector and peers on a book value basis.

Simply Wall St also uses a “Fair Ratio” for P/B, which is a proprietary estimate of what a stock’s multiple could be given factors such as earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks. This is more tailored than a straight peer or industry comparison because it incorporates the company’s own fundamentals rather than assuming it should trade like the average Software stock. There is no Fair Ratio output available here, so it is not possible to say whether Strategy screens as overvalued, undervalued or about right using this metric.

Result: ABOUT RIGHTNasdaqGS:MSTR P/B Ratio as at May 2026

P/B ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Strategy Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St let you connect your view of Strategy’s story to hard numbers by pairing your own forecasts for revenue, earnings, margins and fair value with the current price. A simple Community page tool then shows whether your fair value suggests the stock looks expensive or cheap today, updating that view automatically when new news or earnings arrive. One investor might build a cautious Narrative anchoring on a fair value near US$164.83, while another leans toward the more optimistic fair values of about US$663 or US$705, and each can quickly see, in one place, how their story translates into a clear decision-making framework without needing a spreadsheet.

title: Grok AI is Bullish on MicroStrategy (MSTR) Despite Bitcoin Volatility
content:
We just covered

Grok’s Latest Stock Portfolio in 2026: Elon Musk’s AI Chatbot’s Top 10 Stock Picks. MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) is part of the list (see Grok’s Latest Stock Portfolio in 2026: AI Assistant’s Top 5 Stock Picks).

Number of Hedge Funds: 41

MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) is down 50% over the past year. The stock recently came into the limelight after CEO Michael Saylor indicated potential plans to sell bitcoin to fund dividend payments. That’s a notable shift from his long-standing “never sell” stance on Bitcoin.

But bulls believe MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) is a smart leveraged way to gain exposure to Bitcoin and should be in your portfolio if you think the cryptocurrency will rally in the long term. MSTR trades at a premium to the value of its Bitcoin holdings, measured by mNAV. That premium has risen much higher during past Bitcoin bull markets. Because of this, MSTR shares can sometimes move more sharply than Bitcoin in both rallies and declines.

Bulls also argue that, despite volatility, institutional adoption of Bitcoin is increasing. iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) has grown into one of the largest spot Bitcoin ETFs with tens of billions of dollars in assets under management, alongside rising corporate treasury adoption of Bitcoin.

While we acknowledge the potential of MSTR as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: Here’s What to Know About Strategy Inc (MSTR)’s Bitcoin Holding
content:
Strategy Inc (NASDAQ:MSTR) is one of the most undervalued blockchain stocks to buy now. Strategy Inc (NASDAQ:MSTR) stock has gained more than 40% over the past month.Here’s What to Know About Strategy Inc (MSTR)’s Bitcoin Holding

Strategy Inc reported its Q1 2026 results on May 5. The bitcoin treasury company said its revenue rose 11.9% YoY to $124.3 million. However, its operating loss widened to $14.47 billion from $5.92 billion in the same quarter in 2025. The company said the operating loss included $14.46 billion in unrealized losses tied to its digital assets.

At the end of Q1, Strategy had 818,334 Bitcoin in its holdings, reflecting a 22% increase from the beginning of the year. The company closed the quarter with $2.21 billion in cash and cash equivalents.

While Strategy has preferred accumulating Bitcoin and never selling, the management has indicated openness to selling in the future. According to Strategy Chairman Michael Saylor, they would consider selling some coins if doing so helps increase the company’s Bitcoin per share or improve its capital structure. The management has stated increasing Bitcoin per share as a goal.

Strategy Inc (NASDAQ:MSTR) is a Bitcoin treasury and business software provider. The company was formerly known as MicroStrategy. It acquires and holds Bitcoin as a treasury asset. Other than that, the company provides AI-powered analytics software to enterprise clients.

While we acknowledge the potential of MSTR as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: How Michael Saylor's STRC Causes Mid-Month Bitcoin Rallies
content:
Strategy Inc.(NASDAQ:MSTR) jumped from 4,467 Bitcoin(CRYPTO: BTC) purchases in January to 46,872 BTC in April using STRC preferred stock, creating a mid-month buying pattern that K33 Research says fuels recurring Bitcoin rallies.

STRC Ex-Dividend Date Drives Mid-Month Rallies

Bitcoin posted strong returns during the middle week of March and April.

K33 Head of Research Vetle Lundesaid the pattern could continue this week. STRC pays dividends on the last day of each month, while share ownership gets determined by the ex-dividend date on the 15th.

Strategy issues additional STRC shares via its at-the-market program whenever the stock trades at or above its $100 par value, using the proceeds to buy Bitcoin.

STRC typically trades near par ahead of the ex-dividend date as investors position for payouts, boosting volumes and allowing Strategy to raise more capital.

“This Friday marks another STRC ex-dividend date, and on Monday, May 11, we already saw early signs of the pattern repeating, with STRC recovering to $100 while trading volumes rose to their highest level since April 15,” Lunde said.

STRC Became Primary Driver Of Bitcoin Buys

STRC has become the primary driver of Strategy’s Bitcoin acquisitions this year, rising from 4,467 BTC in January to 22,131 BTC in March and nearly 46,872 BTC in April.

Strategy’s total Bitcoin holdings now stand at 818,869 BTC worth $65.7 billion following Monday’s latest acquisition disclosure.

STRC currently offers an annualized rate of 11.5%, driving investor demand for the product. Strategy recently proposed updating STRC’s dividend payment schedule to twice per month, saying the change could reduce reinvestment lag and enhance liquidity.

Demand May Be Hitting A Ceiling

March and April saw two weeks of substantial Bitcoin accumulation via STRC, but the perpetual preferred stock struggled to trade back to par as quickly this month.

Strategy added just 1 BTC via the instrument, suggesting demand may be hitting a ceiling.

Bitcoin perpetual futures traders remain defensively positioned, with both 7-day and 30-day average funding rates still negative.

The 30-day funding rate is nearing a record-long negative streak of 74 consecutive days.

MSTR Down 2.5% Alongside Bitcoin Dip

Strategy pulled back today after tagging $182.48 intraday, giving back ground alongside Bitcoin’s dip below $80,000. The correlation remains almost perfect.

The recovery structure from April’s $105–$110 lows stays intact. Supertrend at $158.35 and SAR at $165.88 both remain bullish.

The symmetrical triangle breakout from April holds firm, with price retesting the $175–$178 support zone.

Story Continues

title: Strategy Price Prediction: MSTR Bounced 43% in a Month and Our Model Sees 134% Upside
content:
Quick Read

MicroStrategy (MSTR) holds 713,502 BTC as of February 2026 and reported Q4 2025 revenue of $122.99M with subscription services revenue up 62.1%, though a $17.44B unrealized loss under fair value accounting drove EPS to -$42.93. Bitcoin’s price action above $80,000 will determine whether MicroStrategy’s fair value accounting swings between massive gains and losses, making the stock’s valuation almost entirely dependent on crypto market direction. The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and MicroStrategy wasn't one of them. Get them here FREE.

I'll cut straight to the verdict. Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), the Bitcoin treasury vehicle formerly known as MicroStrategy, has been gutted over the past year, but our model sees a meaningful rebound from here.

Current shares trade at $184.42, and the 24/7 Wall St. price target for Strategy is $433.31, implying 134.96% upside over the next 12 months. Our recommendation is buy with moderate confidence of 50%, reflecting the binary nature of MSTR's Bitcoin-linked thesis.

.

The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and MicroStrategy wasn't one of them.Get them here FREE.

24/7 Wall St. Price Target Summary

Metric Value Current Price $184.42 24/7 Wall St. Price Target $433.31 Upside 134.96% Recommendation BUY Confidence Level 50%

A Brutal Year, A Sharp Spring Bounce

Strategy has been one of the most violent rides in large caps. Shares are down 54.45% over the past year from $404.90, but the trend has flipped. MSTR is up 43.36% in the past month and 21.37% year to date, even after a 5.88% single-day drop on May 12. The 52-week range stretches from $104.17 to $457.22.

Q4 2025, reported February 5, was the bruise. Revenue of $122.99M beat by 0.98% and grew 10.74% year over year, but EPS of -$42.93 missed estimates badly thanks to a $17.44B unrealized loss on Bitcoin under ASU 2023-08 fair value accounting. Subscription services revenue grew 62.1%, a quiet bright spot.

Why Bulls See a Breakout Ahead

The bull case is straightforward: Bitcoin goes up, MSTR goes up faster. Strategy holds 713,502 BTC as of February 1, 2026, including 41,002 BTC acquired in January alone. CEO Phong Le pointed to STRC, the flagship Digital Credit instrument, growing to "$3.4 billion in size, supported by increasing liquidity and declining volatility." Capital firepower remains huge: $8.1B on the common ATM and $29B across preferred ATM programs.

Wall Street remains lopsidedly constructive with 14 Buy or Strong Buy ratings against just 1 Hold, and an analyst consensus target of $380.43. Our bull case scenario tops out at $498.30 over 12 months.

Story Continues

Kovaliova Anastasia / Shutterstock.com

What Could Go Wrong

The bear case is equally simple: if Bitcoin tanks, the fair value accounting hammer falls again. Q4 2025's $17.44B unrealized loss showed how unforgiving ASU 2023-08 can be. Management's reaffirmed FY2025 guidance of $80 diluted EPS assumed Bitcoin reaching $150,000 by year-end, which did not happen. Perpetual preferred dividend obligations, the STRC rate of 11.25%, and constant ATM dilution are real costs.

It should be noted that bulls counter the optics: the Q4 loss is non-cash, software revenue accelerated to +10.74% growth, and shareholders equity sits at $44.12B. Our bear case still lands at $331.44, well above today's price.

The Setup From Here, With Eyes Open

The 24/7 Wall St. price target of $433.31, the buy rating, and 50% confidence reflect a coiled spring. The tipping factor for me is the analyst skew combined with the recent month's 43.36% bounce off the lows. The thesis hinges on conviction that Bitcoin holds above $80,000. Investors who cannot stomach beta of 3.6 swings will find the volatility difficult to absorb.

Year 24/7 Wall St. Price Target 2026 $433.31 2030 $1,951

These projections assume Strategy continues compounding Bitcoin per share and that BTC trends higher through the cycle. Significant downside could result from a prolonged crypto winter or forced deleveraging of the preferred stack.
